content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Found 2 correctness issues: exhausted optional panel members currently shrink quorum too aggressively, and stdout `is_error` envelopes on successful exits still bypass the new backend-exhaustion classification.

## Amendments

1. Keep the configured quorum when exhausted members were only excess/optional slots. In `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6880-6894` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:197-199,260-283`, and again in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7135-7149` plus `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:296-299,334-336,909-913,1133-1135`, the code computes `effective_min = min - exhausted`. That is only correct when the panel had no slack. For a panel with 3 members and `min=2`, exhausting the optional 3rd member currently lowers the threshold to 1, so a single completer/reviewer can decide the stage even though the original minimum is still satisfiable with the 2 remaining members. The effective minimum needs to stay at the configured minimum until exhaustion actually removes the last available slack slot.
   Rationale: This changes stage outcomes: completion can return `Complete` with one vote, and final review can restart/accept amendments with one reviewer, even though the configuration still required two participants.
2. Handle stdout error envelopes in the success-exit path of `src/adapters/process_backend.rs`. The new exhaustion classifier only runs on non-zero exits (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1418-1480`), but `finish()` for Claude still ignores `is_error` envelopes on a zero exit (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:145-205`). Because `extract_stdout_error` already recognizes that envelope format (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1614-1636`), a backend that returns `{"is_error":true,"result":"You've hit your usage limit"}` while exiting 0 will still be turned into a `SchemaValidationFailure` retry instead of `BackendExhausted`, so the run will not degrade gracefully for that backend.
   Rationale: The requirement was to classify persistent exhaustion from stderr/stdout. Right now one of the adapter’s supported stdout error formats still bypasses the new non-retryable path.
